caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: 5411 role mix is law-office dominated, not a title-agency census.

inputs.pi_moat: Do not count licensing as labor-task replaceability.
inputs.t50_years: Mortgage cycle and county record quality vary.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No direct AI adoption survey reopened.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Do not infer succession shortage from consolidation.
inputs.active_consolidators: Not a published agency-acquirer census.
inputs.buy_mult: The cited sample is a Southeast residential agency with $620K EBITDA, below the dataset's broad $1–10M EBITDA target band.
inputs.buy_mult: The source is a valuation guide with a sample transaction, not a comprehensive disclosed-deal database.
inputs.exit_mult: This is a conservative no-expansion exit estimate, not an observed future sale.
inputs.exit_mult: The cited sample is below the target EBITDA band and does not establish a time-series exit premium.
S1, S3 and S4 provide industry context but no direct title-agency AI automation or adoption measurement; V and A remain disclosed estimates.
The estimate of six active consolidators is inferred from underwriter market structure, not verified by an agency-acquirer census.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $168,353 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541191); class <5: 4,072 firms, avg revenue/firm $292,418 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $45,763 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 1,314 firms, avg revenue/firm $898,276 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $140,580 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 724 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,931,006 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $302,202 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 527 firms, avg revenue/firm $5,806,233 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $908,676 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 102 firms, avg revenue/firm $14,974,725 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,343,545 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 64 firms, avg revenue/firm $64,930,969 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $10,161,697 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 102

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI primarily replaces structured search, document and administrative tasks; title risk and closing accountability remain.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541191 ($4,182,567,257) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541191 ($12,511,998,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: All deterministic mechanics and exact-artifact checks pass. Every registered source is accessible; the central market, regulatory, attorney-opinion and valuation figures are present on the cited pages. The remaining weaknesses are disclosed AI/adoption and consolidation proxies, plus the thirteen authored caveats. The attorney-opinion mechanism is used for present order-flow ownership in C and long-run paid-demand pressure in the terminal classification, not as a demonstrated thesis-changing double count; in any event the independently supported no-expansion assumption sets M to zero and determines the kill verdict. The record is useful and publishable with caveats.

sources.title: ALTA Reports 2025 Market Share and Title Insurance Premium Volume
sources.what_it_supports: ALTA reports $18.5B of 2025 title premiums, up 13.8% from 2024.

sources.title: Texas title-insurance rate rule adoption
sources.what_it_supports: Texas commissioner fixes/promulgates title premium rates; 2026 order reduced basic rates 6.2%.

sources.title: Fannie Mae Attorney Title Opinion Letter Requirements
sources.what_it_supports: Fannie Mae authorizes qualifying attorney title opinion letters in lieu of title insurance.

sources.title: ALTA Industry Financial Data
sources.what_it_supports: ALTA publishes 2025 family/company/state market-share data and underwriter financial statements.

sources.title: Title & Escrow Company EBITDA Multiples: 3.0x–5.5x — What Buyers Pay (2026)
sources.what_it_supports: The title-and-escrow guide reports a Southeast residential title-agency sample at $2.6M price and $620K EBITDA (4.2x) and states a 3.0x–5.5x EBITDA range for independent title agencies.
